NVDA.title: 5,133 Shares in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM Bought by Collaborative Fund Advisors LLC
NVDA.summary: Collaborative Fund Advisors LLC has acquired a new stake of 5,133 shares in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM) during the first quarter, valued at approximately $1.735 million. This purchase is part of broader institutional interest, with several other hedge funds also recently adding small positions in the semiconductor giant. TSMC has demonstrated strong financial performance, reporting $3.11 EPS and $30.65 billion in revenue for the quarter, and has increased its quarterly dividend to $1.1136 per share, reflecting analyst confidence and robust demand driven by AI and advanced chip technologies.

NVDA.title: DGS Capital Management LLC Has $9.30 Million Holdings in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. $TSM
NVDA.summary: DGS Capital Management LLC increased its stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Ltd. (TSM) by 8.6% in the first quarter, now holding 27,531 shares valued at approximately $9.3 million, making it their 9th largest position. The article highlights TSMC's strong financial results with $3.11 EPS and $30.65 billion revenue, a raised quarterly dividend of $1.1136 per share, and a consensus "Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $449.38. It also notes insider buying and TSMC's ongoing expansion of 3-nanometer capacity across multiple locations.

NVDA.title: Singapore Files New Charges in Nvidia Chip Fraud Case
NVDA.summary: Singaporean prosecutors have filed additional charges, including money laundering, against Alan Wei Zhaolun, a key suspect in an AI server fraud case involving the illicit diversion of Nvidia Corp. chips. Wei pleaded not guilty, and his bail was revoked after prosecutors raised the amount to over S$1.2 million. The case highlights efforts to prevent advanced US chip technology from reaching locations like China through Southeast Asia, with other individuals and a S$55 million mansion purchase also under investigation.

NVDA.title: Singapore Files New Charges in Nvidia Chip Fraud Case
NVDA.summary: Singaporean prosecutors have filed additional charges, including money laundering, against Alan Wei Zhaolun, a key suspect in an AI server fraud case involving Nvidia Corp. chips. This action is part of Singapore's increased efforts to curb the illicit diversion of these chips. Wei Zhaolun pleaded not guilty, and his bail has been revoked, with the amount raised to over S$1.2 million.

NVDA.title: Anthropic’s Claude Available in Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) Foundry Powered by Nvidia GPUs
NVDA.summary: Anthropic's Claude models are now generally available in Microsoft Foundry, allowing organizations to run Claude within their Azure environments, leveraging Microsoft's authentication, billing, and governance. These deployments are hosted in Azure infrastructure powered by Nvidia's GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs. This collaboration among Microsoft, Nvidia, and Anthropic aims to simplify the transition from AI experimentation to production for enterprises building agentic applications.

NVDA.title: Is Intel (INTC) Quietly Recasting Its AI Strategy Around Classrooms And The Edge?
NVDA.summary: Intel (INTC) is expanding its AI strategy beyond data centers by focusing on AI-ready education hardware for classrooms and edge computing. This move, highlighted by a partnership with ASUS, aims to integrate AI into privacy-sensitive environments. While an important step for its edge AI narrative, investors are still more focused on Intel's upcoming earnings report for progress on foundry losses and data center AI demand.

NVDA.title: SK Telecom has received government security certification for the infrastructure of the artificial i..
NVDA.summary: SK Telecom announced that its AI data center infrastructure "Haein" cluster has received Cloud Service Security Certification (CSAP) from KISA. This certification allows SK Telecom to enter the AI business in public and financial sectors and accelerate its advance into the Sovereign AI cloud market. The Haein cluster, based on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, is the first AI cloud operator in Korea to obtain CSAP certification for this technology.

NVDA.title: Self-Driving Startup Turing Gets AMD Backing, Adopts AMD GPUs
NVDA.summary: Self-driving technology developer Turing Inc. has secured backing from AMD Ventures and integrated AMD's AI accelerators into its systems, diversifying its hardware reliance previously centered on Nvidia. The Japanese startup, valued at $600 million after a recent funding extension, aims to launch its software for consumer markets and robotaxis by 2028, prioritizing cost-effectiveness in the capital-intensive autonomous driving sector. Despite a delayed market entry compared to some rivals, Turing's executives believe their advanced technology and cost-efficient approach will allow them to achieve success, especially in the crucial Japanese automotive industry.

MSFT.title: CUSHING ASSET MANAGEMENT LP dba NXG INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT Has $32.81 Million Holdings in Phillips 66 $PSX
MSFT.summary: CUSHING ASSET MANAGEMENT LP dba NXG INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT reduced its stake in Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) by 5.7% in the first quarter, now holding 180,100 shares valued at $32.81 million. Despite the reduction, Phillips 66 remains the firm's 17th largest holding, constituting 1.6% of its portfolio. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Phillips 66, with an average price target of $192.72, and the company recently posted better-than-expected earnings and paid a quarterly dividend of $1.27 per share.

MSFT.title: 8,755 Shares in Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. $LYV Acquired by Elevation Point Wealth Partners LLC
MSFT.summary: Elevation Point Wealth Partners LLC recently acquired 8,755 shares of Live Nation Entertainment (NYSE:LYV) worth approximately $1.335 million during the first quarter. Institutional investors collectively own a significant 74.52% of the company's stock, with several large funds increasing their holdings. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with an average price target of $188.50, despite the company missing EPS estimates but exceeding revenue expectations in its latest quarterly report.

MSFT.title: Baer Investment Advisory LLC Cuts Position in Analog Devices, Inc. $ADI
MSFT.summary: Baer Investment Advisory LLC significantly reduced its stake in Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) by 96.1% in the first quarter, leaving it with 710 shares worth $226,000. Despite this, Analog Devices reported strong quarterly results with EPS of $3.09 and revenue of $3.62 billion, exceeding analyst expectations. The company maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from Wall Street analysts, with several raising price targets.

MSFT.title: AI Stocks Retail Investors Are Watching in Broadcom ServiceNow and Alphabet
MSFT.summary: This article examines three AI stocks – Broadcom (AVGO), ServiceNow (NOW), and Alphabet (GOOGL) – that are attracting retail investor attention due to their pivotal roles in the AI transformation. Broadcom is highlighted for its semiconductor and networking infrastructure, ServiceNow for its cloud platform streamlining enterprise AI deployment, and Alphabet for its combination of a profitable ad business with a fast-growing cloud and AI platform, while also noting potential risks like debt, valuation, and regulatory scrutiny for each.

MSFT.title: MSFT Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Microsoft Corporation Securities Fraud Lawsuit
MSFT.summary: Rosen Law Firm reminds Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT) investors who purchased common stock between May 1, 2025, and January 28, 2026, of the August 11, 2026, lead plaintiff deadline for a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Microsoft made false and misleading statements regarding its Copilot products, AI model performance, and necessary capital expenditures. Investors impacted by these alleged misrepresentations may be entitled to compensation.

MSFT.title: AI: Microsoft 'Sum of the Parts' (SOTP) Spin-Off case. AI-RTZ #1139
MSFT.summary: This article discusses the potential for a spin-off of Microsoft's business units based on a "sum-of-the-parts" valuation, suggesting its true value could be significantly higher than its current market capitalization. It highlights investor concerns regarding AI spending ROI and software disruption, while also pointing to Microsoft's strong embedded software presence and the growth of its Copilot AI. The author suggests that the AI-driven "bundling and unbundling" trend could lead to similar spin-off considerations for other large tech companies.

MSFT.title: Anthropic’s Claude Available in Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) Foundry Powered by Nvidia GPUs
MSFT.summary: Anthropic's Claude models are now generally available in Microsoft Foundry, allowing organizations to run Claude within their Azure environments, leveraging Microsoft's authentication, billing, and governance. These deployments are hosted in Azure infrastructure powered by Nvidia's GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs. This collaboration among Microsoft, Nvidia, and Anthropic aims to simplify the transition from AI experimentation to production for enterprises building agentic applications.

MSFT.title: About one-third of Coloradans use AI, Microsoft report finds
MSFT.summary: A Microsoft report indicates that about one-third of Coloradans use artificial intelligence tools, slightly surpassing the national average. The study highlights uneven AI adoption across the U.S., with higher usage in metropolitan, college, and knowledge-based economic areas. Factors influencing adoption include internet access, workforce composition, and public trust, with Boulder County leading Colorado in AI user share.

GOOGL.title: Berkshire Hathaway Amasses $41 Billion Stake in Alphabet, Signaling a New Forever Holding
GOOGL.summary: Berkshire Hathaway has rapidly accumulated a roughly $41 billion stake in Alphabet, making it the conglomerate's fourth-largest equity holding and surpassing its iconic Coca-Cola position. This significant investment, which included a $10 billion private placement to fund Alphabet's AI infrastructure, reflects Berkshire's classic criteria for durable competitive moats, strong free cash flow generation, and fair valuation. The move signals that Berkshire, under Greg Abel, views Alphabet as a potential "forever holding," akin to American Express and Apple, aligning with its long-term investment philosophy.

GOOGL.title: AI Stocks Retail Investors Are Watching in Broadcom ServiceNow and Alphabet
GOOGL.summary: This article examines three AI stocks – Broadcom (AVGO), ServiceNow (NOW), and Alphabet (GOOGL) – that are attracting retail investor attention due to their pivotal roles in the AI transformation. Broadcom is highlighted for its semiconductor and networking infrastructure, ServiceNow for its cloud platform streamlining enterprise AI deployment, and Alphabet for its combination of a profitable ad business with a fast-growing cloud and AI platform, while also noting potential risks like debt, valuation, and regulatory scrutiny for each.

It highlights investor concerns regarding AI spending ROI and software disruption, while also pointing to Microsoft's strong embedded software presence and the growth of its Copilot AI. The author suggests that the AI-driven "bundling and unbundling" trend could lead to similar spin-off considerations for other large tech companies.

GOOGL.title: Nvidia supplier Hon Hai’s sales beat on continued AI demand
GOOGL.summary: Nvidia supplier Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. (Foxconn) reported a stronger-than-expected 40% increase in quarterly sales, driven by sustained demand for AI-related products. The company anticipates continued momentum in AI server shipments and overall growth quarter-on-quarter and year-on-year, despite facing a shortage of memory chips. This performance comes as major tech companies like Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft are significantly increasing their AI investments.

GOOGL.title: Stephens Investment Management Group LLC Sells 140,341 Shares of Ciena Corporation $CIEN
GOOGL.summary: Stephens Investment Management Group LLC reduced its stake in Ciena Corporation by 45.8% in Q1 2026, selling 140,341 shares, but still holds 166,171 shares valued at $64.5 million. Ciena recently surpassed earnings expectations with $1.64 EPS and $1.57 billion in revenue, a 39.5% increase year-over-year. Wall Street maintains a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $530.56, following multiple analysts raising their targets.

GOOGL.title: $Tradr 2X Long CRWV Daily ETF (CWVX.US)$
GOOGL.summary: This article from the Moomoo community features a post about the Tradr 2X Long CRWV Daily ETF (CWVX.US). The post was made by user loloshan and highlights featured tech stocks like SpaceX, Intel, ASML Holding, Disney, and Alphabet-A, noting their strong market presence and influence.

GOOGL.title: KKR & Co. Inc. $KKR Shares Bought by Fjarde AP Fonden Fourth Swedish National Pension Fund
GOOGL.summary: Fjarde AP Fonden Fourth Swedish National Pension Fund increased its stake in KKR & Co. Inc. by 54.8% in the first quarter, acquiring 53,700 additional shares to own a total of 151,675 shares valued at $14.03 million. KKR & Co. Inc. recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.195 per share and approved a $500 million share buyback program. Despite analyst price target reductions, the stock maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $134.53.

GOOGL.title: Louisiana State Employees Retirement System Purchases New Shares in VeriSign, Inc. $VRSN
GOOGL.summary: The Louisiana State Employees Retirement System has acquired 4,200 shares of VeriSign, Inc. (NASDAQ:VRSN) for approximately $1.043 million during the first quarter. This purchase contributes to the already high institutional ownership of 92.9% for VeriSign shares. The company recently reported strong quarterly results, surpassing EPS expectations with $2.34 per share and revenue of $428.9 million, leading analysts to rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a target price of $327.67.
